title: Professional Training & Capacity Building
text:
We empower teams to confidently navigate complex spatial challenges through hands‑on, applied training programs.



Workshops on point pattern analysis, spatial regression, and interpolation

Practical exercises using real‑world environmental datasets

Guidance on selecting appropriate statistical methods





Training in predictive modeling and habitat suitability analysis

Species distribution modeling using modern computational tools

Best practices for model evaluation and interpretation





Custom instruction in R and Python for GIS workflows

Advanced raster analysis and automated mapping

Building reproducible geospatial scripts and applications

Data Cleaning & Preprocessing
Spatial data is only as good as its foundation. We ensure your datasets are robust and analysis-ready.

title: What We Offer

text:
We combine remote sensing, ecological modeling, and social-ecological systems research to answer one question for donors, conservancies, and governments across the region: is this land-use or climate claim actually true — and can we prove it over time?


View services Start a project






Services


Each service reads like a layer in a GIS project — independently useful, and stronger together. Pick one, or let us build the full stack for a project.






LAYER 01




Independent, satellite-grounded MRV for carbon and biodiversity credit projects

We build and audit measurement-reporting-verification pipelines — biomass estimation, land-cover change detection, time-series monitoring — to registry standard, so credits hold up to scrutiny and due diligence holds up to buyers.


Who it’s for
Project developers Conservancies Impact investors

02°10’S 38°04’E
Illustrative anchor: Tsavo–Kasigau region











LAYER 02




Mapping and forecasting how agriculture, settlement, and forest cover are moving

Historical change detection and scenario modeling for deforestation, agricultural expansion, and urban growth — built for spatial planning, safeguard compliance, and donor reporting.


Who it’s for
County governments Land-use planners Conservation NGOs

00°49’S 35°42’E
Illustrative anchor: Mau Forest Complex







Aerial view of forest edge and farmland near the Mau Forest Complex








LAYER 03




Habitat fragmentation, wildlife corridors, and human-wildlife conflict — mapped and modeled

Connectivity analysis and protected-area effectiveness assessments that show where conservation investment and community agreements will actually hold, not just where a map says they should.


Who it’s for
Wildlife conservancies Park authorities Conservation NGOs

00°02’N 37°33’E
Illustrative anchor: Northern Rangelands







Wildlife corridor landscape in Kenya’s Northern Rangelands








LAYER 04




Where land, climate, and community governance meet — analyzed together, not separately

We pair spatial data with social science to assess resilience, land governance, and community-based natural resource management — the layer most geospatial firms skip, and most funders now expect.


Who it’s for
Research consortia Bilateral donors Conservancy associations

03°04’S 37°21’E
Illustrative anchor: Kilimanjaro socio-ecological zone







Community grazing land near Kilimanjaro’s socio-ecological zone








LAYER 05




Drought, rangeland, and yield forecasting for the people who plan around them

Spatiotemporal modeling of drought risk, pastoral resilience, and agricultural yield, built to support early-warning systems and index-based insurance design.


Who it’s for
Insurers & reinsurers Early-warning programs Agtech

02°17’N 37°59’E
Illustrative anchor: Marsabit, Northern Kenya







Dry rangeland landscape near Marsabit, Northern Kenya











Approach






A


Every model is checked against ground truth — plots, transects, or partner verification — not just pixels.



B


Social-ecological methods mean local governance and knowledge shape the analysis, not just the output.



C


Work is documented to the standard carbon registries, donors, and auditors actually require — no rework at review time.








Sectors




Carbon & Conservation Finance Government & County Planning Multilaterals & Bilateral Donors Research Institutions Agriculture & Insurance






Tell us what you’re trying to prove, protect, or plan for. We’ll tell you which layers apply.


EMAIL
hello@equatorgeospatial.com
BASED IN
Nairobi, Kenya — serving East Africa

title: About Us
text:
About Us



Prime Geospatial was founded in 2018 to close the gap between complex spatial data science and real-world environmental decisions — built by scientists who got tired of disconnected data and prohibitive travel costs standing between good analysis and the ground it describes.







Prime Geospatial founders at the Christmas get-together where the idea began








Who We Are





Prime Geospatial began as a collaborative effort among three long-time friends and doctoral researchers pursuing PhDs in Geography, Spatial Modeling, Geospatial Science, and Climate Science at Michigan State University and The Pennsylvania State University.

Three doctoral researchers. One shared frustration. One Christmas conversation that started it all.


Read the full story →Show less ↑

The idea took shape at a Christmas get-together, born out of a shared frustration: navigating disconnected, multi-source data and facing prohibitive travel costs just to validate study areas on the ground. We kept coming back to the same question — why should an organization need an advanced degree in GIS, or an enormous travel budget, to actually use its own spatial data?








Our Approach





Geospatial AI has made data more abundant than ever — but abundance isn’t the same as understanding. Purely computer-driven analyses often miss localized geographic nuance, and when global travel isn’t possible, as the pandemic made clear, digital models can drift further from reality.



Raw Computing


Accessing, processing, and analyzing complex multi-source datasets — the heavy lifting automated models are good at.



→


Ground Truth


Expert interpretation and field validation — the context and judgment automated models leave out.





Read more about our approach →Show less ↑

Our approach is grounded in the belief that spatial modeling should be reproducible, robust, and scientifically sound. Whether we're analyzing landscape patterns or assessing environmental risk, we interpret data within the context of landscape ecology theory and statistical integrity — not just process it.




Remote Sensing Landscape & Forest Ecology Spatiotemporal Modeling Ecological Modeling Land-Use Mapping & Modeling Social-Ecological Systems






How We Work






STEP 01


Ground the question in solid ecological and geospatial science first.



→


STEP 02


Adapt methods to the specific landscape, dataset, and decision at hand.



→


STEP 03


Turn the analysis into something a client’s team can actually use.



→


STEP 04


Hand off decision-ready insight — verified, documented, and defensible.








Core Values






01


Spatial modeling should be reproducible, robust, and scientifically sound — every time, not just when it’s convenient.



02


Computation alone isn’t enough. We pair it with field validation and local context automated models miss.



03


Clients get not just answers, but an understanding of the spatial processes behind them.



04


Global, purely computer-driven analyses overlook localized nuance. We don’t.



05


We train the wider environmental and geospatial community, not just our own clients.








Who We Serve







Translating complex multi-source data into standardized, actionable planning frameworks for sustainable development decisions.



Rigorous, localized data-cleaning pipelines that keep costs down without cutting corners on quality.



Clean, verified, ready-to-analyze datasets that pair rapid digital modeling with real-world validation.



Removing the operational overhead of in-house data processing, so commercial teams can move faster.







Our Team










Founder photo placeholder





Co-Founder — Geospatial Data Science







Founder photo placeholder





Co-Founder — Remote Sensing







Founder photo placeholder





Co-Founder — Landscape & Forest Ecology







Founder photo placeholder





Co-Founder — Social-Ecological Systems








Capacity Building





We offer professional training in spatial statistics and ecological modeling to help practitioners across the environmental and geospatial community take on increasingly difficult environmental challenges — because a stronger field benefits everyone in it.







Tell us what you’re trying to prove, protect, or plan for. We’ll tell you which layers apply.


EMAIL
hello@primegeospatial.com
BASED IN
Nairobi, Kenya — serving East Africa
Send us a brief





© 2026 PRIME GEOSPATIAL
NAIROBI, KENYA

title: About Us
text:
About Us



Prime Geospatial was founded in 2018 to close the gap between complex spatial data science and real-world environmental decisions — built by scientists who got tired of disconnected data and prohibitive travel costs standing between good analysis and the ground it describes.







Who We Are





Prime Geospatial began as a collaborative effort among three long-time friends and doctoral researchers pursuing PhDs in Geography, Spatial Modeling, Geospatial Science, and Climate Science at Michigan State University and The Pennsylvania State University.
The idea took shape at a Christmas get-together, born out of a shared frustration: navigating disconnected, multi-source data and facing prohibitive travel costs just to validate study areas on the ground. We kept coming back to the same question — why should an organization need an advanced degree in GIS, or an enormous travel budget, to actually use its own spatial data?






Our Approach





Geospatial AI has made data more abundant than ever — but abundance isn’t the same as understanding. Purely computer-driven analyses often miss localized geographic nuance, and when global travel isn’t possible, as the pandemic made clear, digital models can drift further from reality. We handle the heavy lifting of accessing, processing, and analyzing complex datasets, while providing the expert interpretation and ground-truthing that automated models leave out.
Our approach is grounded in the belief that spatial modeling should be reproducible, robust, and scientifically sound. Whether we’re analyzing landscape patterns or assessing environmental risk, we interpret data within the context of landscape ecology theory and statistical integrity — not just process it.


Remote Sensing Landscape & Forest Ecology Spatiotemporal Modeling Ecological Modeling Land-Use Mapping & Modeling Social-Ecological Systems






Who We Serve







Translating complex multi-source data into standardized, actionable planning frameworks for sustainable development decisions.



Rigorous, localized data-cleaning pipelines that keep costs down without cutting corners on quality.



Clean, verified, ready-to-analyze datasets that pair rapid digital modeling with real-world validation.



Removing the operational overhead of in-house data processing, so commercial teams can move faster.







Capacity Building





We offer professional training in spatial statistics and ecological modeling to help practitioners across the environmental and geospatial community take on increasingly difficult environmental challenges — because a stronger field benefits everyone in it.

title: About Us
text: Tell us what you’re trying to prove, protect, or plan for. We’ll tell you which layers apply.

title: Who We Are
text:
Who We Are
Prime Geospatial was founded to bring rigorous landscape ecology metrics, robust data cleaning, and reproducible analytical pipelines directly to environmental planning challenges. We transform complex spatial observations into clear, data-driven frameworks.





Our Philosophy
At Prime Geospatial, our mission is to elevate the standard of spatial data science through technical rigor, transparency, and advanced ecological modeling. We believe that environmental decision-making is only as strong as the data behind it—and the expertise applied to interpret that data.


Technical Foundation
The foundation of Prime Geospatial is built upon years of academic and professional engagement with cutting-edge spatial technologies. We do not just process data; we interpret it within the context of landscape ecology theory and statistical integrity.





Our Core Competencies





Spatial Data Science
Advanced R and Python workflows for raster analysis and complex data visualization.





Ecological Modeling
Theoretical landscape ecology, focusing on the critical distinction between configuration and spatial patterns.





Statistical Rigor

Expertise in spatial statistics and reproducible research practices to ensure scientific integrity.

title: Data Cleaning & Preprocessing
text:
Spatial analysis is only as reliable as the data behind it. We ensure your datasets are accurate, consistent, and ready for high‑quality analysis.



Merging spatial and non‑spatial datasets from multiple sources

Harmonizing formats, projections, and attribute structures

Resolving conflicts between overlapping or redundant datasets





Identifying and removing errors, noise, and inconsistencies

Validating geometries, attributes, and spatial relationships

Ensuring compliance with analytical and regulatory standards





Building reproducible workflows for automated data preparation

Standardizing transformations, filtering, and feature engineering

Preparing analysis‑ready datasets for modeling and visualization

section: Turning landscape data into trusted intelligence for sustainable futures.
text: Turning landscape data into trusted intelligence for sustainable futures.

Prime Geospatial
PrimeGeo is a science-driven spatial analytics and environmental intelligence firm.
We transform complex landscape data into actionable evidence that helps governments, conservation organizations, investors, project developers, and communities make confident decisions. By integrating satellite observation, advanced spatial modeling, field-based data, and social-ecological insights, we deliver audit-ready intelligence that connects environmental outcomes with real-world impact.
Built on a foundation of scientific rigor, transparency, and local relevance, we work at the intersection of conservation, climate, and sustainable development. Our expertise spans carbon measurement and verification, land-use change modeling, landscape ecology, resilience assessment, and evidence-based planning. We bridge spatial and social science, combining ecological data, community knowledge, and governance dynamics to provide a complete understanding of how landscapes function and change over time.
From project design and baseline assessments to monitoring, verification, and impact evaluation, we help our partners move beyond assumptions and make decisions backed by credible, measurable evidence.

section: Five layers of analysis — one landscape.

section: Turning landscape data into trusted intelligence for sustainable futures.
text:
Turning landscape data into trusted intelligence for sustainable futures.

PrimeGeo is a science-driven spatial analytics and environmental intelligence firm.
We transform complex landscape data into actionable evidence that helps governments, conservation organizations, investors, project developers, and communities make confident decisions. By integrating satellite observation, advanced spatial modeling, field-based data, and social-ecological insights, we deliver audit-ready intelligence that connects environmental outcomes with real-world impact.
Built on a foundation of scientific rigor, transparency, and local relevance, we work at the intersection of conservation, climate, and sustainable development. Our expertise spans carbon measurement and verification, land-use change modeling, landscape ecology, resilience assessment, and evidence-based planning. We bridge spatial and social science, combining ecological data, community knowledge, and governance dynamics to provide a complete understanding of how landscapes function and change over time.
From project design and baseline assessments to monitoring, verification, and impact evaluation, we help our partners move beyond assumptions and make decisions backed by credible, measurable evidence.


Learn more about us Learn more about the team

title: Prime Geospatial
text: Welcome to Prime Geospatial. After 8 years of dormancy, we are back to solve your most complex spatiotemporal and environmental data challenges. We specialize in reproducible workflows, data cleaning, and custom spatial analytics.

title: Prime Geospatial
text:
Interactive Proof of Concept
Below is a live demonstration of our web-mapping deployment pipelines, rendered directly from spatial data attributes.

title: Projects & Applications
text: Example, activities, projects, applications go here

We combine remote sensing, ecological modeling, and social-ecological systems research to answer one question for donors, conservancies, and governments across the region: is this land-use or climate claim actually true — and can we prove it over time?


View services Start a project








What We Offer


Each service reads like a layer in a GIS project — independently useful, and stronger together. Pick one, or let us build the full stack for a project.






LAYER 01




Independent, satellite-grounded MRV for carbon and biodiversity credit projects

We build and audit measurement-reporting-verification pipelines — biomass estimation, land-cover change detection, time-series monitoring — to registry standard, so credits hold up to scrutiny and due diligence holds up to buyers.











LAYER 02




Mapping and forecasting how agriculture, settlement, and forest cover are moving

Historical change detection and scenario modeling for deforestation, agricultural expansion, and urban growth — built for spatial planning, safeguard compliance, and donor reporting.



Who it’s for
County governments Land-use planners Conservation NGOs

00°49’S 35°42’E
Illustrative anchor: Mau Forest Complex







LAYER 03




Habitat fragmentation, wildlife corridors, and human-wildlife conflict — mapped and modeled

Connectivity analysis and protected-area effectiveness assessments that show where conservation investment and community agreements will actually hold, not just where a map says they should.



Who it’s for
Wildlife conservancies Park authorities Conservation NGOs

00°02’N 37°33’E
Illustrative anchor: Northern Rangelands







LAYER 04




Where land, climate, and community governance meet — analyzed together, not separately

We pair spatial data with social science to assess resilience, land governance, and community-based natural resource management — the layer most geospatial firms skip, and most funders now expect.



Who it’s for
Research consortia Bilateral donors Conservancy associations

03°04’S 37°21’E
Illustrative anchor: Kilimanjaro socio-ecological zone







LAYER 05




Drought, rangeland, and yield forecasting for the people who plan around them

Spatiotemporal modeling of drought risk, pastoral resilience, and agricultural yield, built to support early-warning systems and index-based insurance design.



Who it’s for
Insurers & reinsurers Early-warning programs Agtech

02°17’N 37°59’E
Illustrative anchor: Marsabit, Northern Kenya










Approach






A


Every model is checked against ground truth — plots, transects, or partner verification — not just pixels.



B


Social-ecological methods mean local governance and knowledge shape the analysis, not just the output.



C


Work is documented to the standard carbon registries, donors, and auditors actually require — no rework at review time.








Sectors




Carbon & Conservation Finance Government & County Planning Multilaterals & Bilateral Donors Research Institutions Agriculture & Insurance






Tell us what you’re trying to prove, protect, or plan for. We’ll tell you which layers apply.


EMAIL
hello@equatorgeospatial.com
BASED IN
Nairobi, Kenya — serving East Africa

title: Bridging the gap between data and discovery with Prime Geospatial
text:
Bridging the gap between data and discovery with Prime Geospatial
Geospatial technology provides access to data from layers of site-specific and remote sensing arrays. We harness this data to bring deep analytical precision to your workflows, combat environmental fragmentation, and engineer reproducible data pipelines.




→ What We Offer






Geospatial Consulting


We offer tailored strategies for site-specific and regional environmental challenges while aligning data frameworks with your core technical objectives.



Landscape Analysis: Assessing spatial patterns and environmental change over time.
Environmental Risk Assessment: Leveraging spatial intelligence to mitigate operational risks.
Project Strategy: Integration geospatial technology into your existing workflows.


Learn More













Data Collection, Cleaning & Preprocessing


Spatial data is only as good as its foundation. We build automated QA/QC workflows to fix messy coordinates, handle projection shifts, validate topology, and ensure your datasets are completely robust and analysis-ready.



Data Integration: Merging disparate spatial and non-spatial environmental datasets.
Cleaning & Quality Control: Systematic removal of errors, noise, and inconsistencies in spatial features.
Preprocessing Pipelines: Developing reproducible workflows for automated data preparation.


Learn More






Analytical Support


Beyond data preparation, we offer deep-dive analytical services for specialized research.



Statistical Modeling: Building custom models to address unique ecological questions.
Visualization: Creating high-impact, data-driven visualizations for reporting and decision-making.
Code Review & Optimization: Refining your existing spatial scripts for speed, scalability, and reproducibility.


Learn More












Professional Training & Capacity Building


We empower your team to handle complex spatial challenges with confidence. Through targeted workshops and curated technical materials, we help transfer advanced spatial skills directly to your internal workflows.



Spatial Statistics: Hands-on workshops covering modern methods for point pattern analysis, spatial regression, and interpolation.
Ecological Modeling: Technical courses on predictive modeling, habitat suitability, and species distribution.
Geospatial Application: Custom training in R/Python for GIS, advanced raster analysis, and automated mapping.


Learn More





Have a specific project in mind?

Contact Us

title: Comprehensive Geospatial & Analytical Solutions
text:
At Prime Geospatial, we provide specialized services designed to bridge the gap between raw environmental data and actionable decision-making. We combine statistical rigor with practical ecological application.


1. Geospatial Consulting
We offer tailored strategies for site-specific and regional environmental challenges. - Landscape Analysis: Assessing spatial patterns and environmental change over time. - Environmental Risk Assessment: Leveraging spatial intelligence to mitigate operational risks. - Project Strategy: Advising on the integration of geospatial technology into your existing workflows.


2. Data Cleaning & Preprocessing
Spatial data is only as good as its foundation. We ensure your datasets are robust and analysis-ready.

Data Integration: Merging disparate spatial and non-spatial environmental datasets.
Cleaning & Quality Control: Systematic removal of errors, noise, and inconsistencies in spatial features.
Preprocessing Pipelines: Developing reproducible workflows for automated data preparation.



3. Professional Training & Capacity Building
We empower your team to handle complex spatial challenges with confidence.

Spatial Statistics: Hands-on workshops covering modern methods for point pattern analysis, spatial regression, and interpolation.
Ecological Modeling: Technical courses on predictive modeling, habitat suitability, and species distribution.
Geospatial Application: Custom training in R/Python for GIS, advanced raster analysis, and automated mapping.



4. Analytical Support
Beyond data preparation, we offer deep-dive analytical services for specialized research.

Statistical Modeling: Building custom models to address unique ecological questions.
Visualization: Creating high-impact, data-driven visualizations for reporting and decision-making.
Code Review & Optimization: Refining your existing spatial scripts for speed, scalability, and reproducibility.





Why Choose Prime Geospatial?

Reproducible Workflows: Every service is delivered with full documentation and open-science standards.
Expert Integration: We combine high-level statistical theory with practical, on-the-ground environmental knowledge.
Tailored Solutions: We don’t believe in “one-size-fits-all” spatial analysis.

Let’s discuss your specific needs. [Contact Us / Request a Consultation]
